name: Mocha Cookies
recipeIngredient: 2 Large egg whites, at room temperature
recipeInstructions:
1. Cream together margarine and sugar until light and fluffy.
2. Add vanilla extract to the mixture and stir until combined.
3. Gradually add the liquid sugar substitute to the creamed mixture and mix lightly.
4. Stir together flour, cocoa powder, instant coffee granules, and baking powder in a separate bowl until well blended.
5. Mix the dry ingredients into the creamed mixture at medium speed for 1-2 minutes until fully incorporated.
6. Add raisins and chopped nuts to the dough and mix gently.
7. Fold in egg whites until they are evenly distributed throughout the dough.
8. Drop spoonfuls of dough onto a baking sheet lined with aluminum foil or sprayed with Pam cooking spray.
9. Using fingers dipped in cold water, shape the dough into round cookies about 1/4 inch thick.
10. Gently spread the dough to ensure an even thickness before baking.
11. Bake at 375°F for about 12 minutes, or until the cookies spring back when lightly touched.
12. Remove from the hot baking sheet and transfer to a wire rack to cool to room temperature.
13. Serve one cookie per serving.
